title: Yui and the Ark ugly split
display_title: Yui and the Ark ugly split
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 05, 2012 2:19 pm Still rebelling against the show, Panther-man! You're relying on the assumption that Gendo will inevitably continue to try to get Yui out of the Eva once the salvage fails, and not doing so makes him an "idiot". But it's clear that Gendo stops trying, for whatever reason, and instead co-opts 3I with hopes that he can go to her. We can probably assume that Gendo is not an idiot, merely desperate to be with his beloved again. He probably settles on the "go to Yui" plan for a good reason. It's also important to remember that Yui and her two compadres had some sort of plan in place before Yui's "accident", and this is presumably still being taken forward at the same time as Gendo's more private ambition. So it's not just a matter of getting Yui back (/being with her again); Gendo, Fuyu, and Yui have to pull off whatever it is they've been planning for some 15 years, as well. So, if you really think Gendo isn't an idiot, then you'll give him enough credit to recognize that maybe (once he knew what was going on) he respected Yui's wishes enough to let her stay in the Eva and do what she needed to do. Plus what MonkEd says too. (In fact, considering what happens to Rei's soul when it gets transferred around...) Bagheera wrote: I don't see any particular reason for Lilith's soul to hop around as is being suggested here, nor any reason to think her Chamber of Guf wasn't empty (because, well, humanity).

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 05, 2012 2:28 pm Reichu wrote: @symbv: This bit here: Quote: Fuyutsuki: The Chamber of Guf has been unsealed... Have the doors to the world's beginning and end finally been opened? ...seems to imply that Lilith's Chamber of Guf has been closed for quite some time. They open at the beginning to release all souls, and they open again at the end to retrieve them.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 05, 2012 2:36 pm Reichu wrote: Still rebelling against the show, Panther-man!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 05, 2012 2:40 pm symbv wrote: You mind providing the Japanese text? The word "unsealed" seems to be a bit odd here.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 05, 2012 2:41 pm ^ ^ OK, none of the above is covered in the Japanese blogs on Eva I read. So I have nothing to add to this discussion that is based on the "Japanese consensus interpretation" What I said is purely opinion from me alone. And I say that we are not sure what happens when an Eva's core is destroyed. ==== ^ 冬月｢ガフの部屋が開く。世界の始まりと終局の扉が―」 So Fuyutsuki only said "The Chamber of Guf has opened". He did not say it is "unsealed". The Japanese bloggers saw it as a way to draw parallel with the 2nd Impact when Adam's Chamber of Guf was opened. Anyway, at least judging from all the dialogues in the show, we have Adam's Chamber of Guf being empty mentioned already and here we have Ritsuko mentioning an empty Chamber of Guf. Not to say closed is not the same as empty... One question: How do we know Eva01 is a Lilith clone?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 05, 2012 2:50 pm Bagheera wrote: However, it's not at all clear to me why the rational parts of that plan require Yui to be stuck in a giant artificial person.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 05, 2012 2:57 pm Reichu wrote: So while it's convoluted as all hell, they've clearly been setting things up so that Yui is right at the middle of 3I and HIP whether Seele like it or not, and she can accordingly influence the outcome in ways that would have been impossible were she not in the body of a god. The principle behind it seems pretty straightforward to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 05, 2012 3:10 pm Bagheera wrote: I would be more inclined to agree if she actually did anything of note during 3I. Perhaps the Lance or Lilith cockblocked her, allowing Lilith to pass the buck to Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 05, 2012 3:15 pm Reichu wrote: Just because she seems passive doesn't mean she isn't an important player. Think outside the box! (And Yui does do the HIP demolition work pretty much by herself.)

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jan 05, 2012 3:20 pm > キール｢約束の時が来た。ロンギヌスの槍を失った、今リリスによる補完はできぬ。 > 唯一、リリスの分身たる、エヴァ初号機による遂行を　願うぞ」 Thanks, I found it too. But Eva-01 is a test prototype and not a production model, right? This also fits with what I said the other day about Eva-01 having part of Lilith in its core before Yui tried her contact experiment. It had a soul but not the production type that were produced under Adam Plan.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 05, 2012 3:28 pm Bagheera wrote: Does she? I thought the MPEs brought her to heel pretty effectively before Lilith showed up.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jan 05, 2012 3:42 pm Reichu wrote: Are we supposed to assume that Ritsuko is not referring to Eva-01 when she says that Evas have salvaged souls? It's confusing because she says that Evas were made from Adam, without mentioning any exceptions. I don't know if the Eva01-Lilith connection is supposed to negate the other things that Ritsuko says about Evas, with respect to their applicability to Eva-01.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 05, 2012 4:16 pm Reichu wrote: I get the sense that she was deliberately letting herself be subdued -- "playing along", if you will -- so that she could hijack HIP from the inside. I don't pretend to know all the details, of course... but the huge manifestation of power that she presents at the end as she rips HIP apart seems rather telling.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jan 05, 2012 4:21 pm Regarding whether or not Yui had any control over the HIP, I would posit that she doesn't need to exert any control over the HIP while it was in progress, because what seems far more important is the influence she exerted before that. Without Yui in the core, how else would Eva-01 have gotten an S2 into itself, for example? Even if that one act was Yui's sole contribution to the ultimate plan, it's a pretty damn big one. EDIT: And now to rebut myself. Why bother involving Eva-01 in the process at all if Lilith is capable of doing everything all by herself? -_-; Back to square one.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 05, 2012 4:25 pm Monk Ed wrote: Regarding whether or not Yui had any control over the HIP, I would posit that she doesn't need to exert any control over the HIP while it was in progress, because what seems far more important is the influence she exerted before that. Without Yui in the core, how else would Eva-01 have gotten an S2 into itself, for example? Even if that one act was Yui's sole contribution to the ultimate plan, it's a pretty damn big one.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jan 05, 2012 4:26 pm Bagheera wrote: How so? It doesn't seem to have mattered a bit. It was obviously essential for her ultimate plan, but for the rest, not so much.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 05, 2012 4:51 pm Bagheera wrote: (...) I find this to be a baffling goal, as the colonization angle makes more sense, but I can't find evidence of the latter in that scene. Perhaps something I missed in the series?

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Thu Jan 05, 2012 10:37 pm Reichu wrote: Whereas Yui floating around in space doing nothing until the Big Freeze serves no purpose whatsoever other than to endure insufferable loneliness in the feeble hope that someone notices her and wonders how she got there. I mean seriously. For somebody who treated life as a precious thing, that's about the most depressing and pointless fate she could go out of her way to secure.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 05, 2012 10:49 pm Fireand'chutes77 wrote: (...) is more (or less) depressing than Yui's Last Stand?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Jan 06, 2012 6:19 am ^ While I am not sure what is in the draft, but when "ark" and "new world" were mentioned in EoE it only seemed to me the meaning is figurative: New world is a new stage of life and Ark is just a conduit/channel/means (Kiel also mentioned there is no need to aim for a new land right after he mentioned Eva as "ark") As for what Yui/Eva01 was doing at the end of EoE, I have never read in Japanese blogs that it has to do with recolonization or repopulation in another planet. Reichu wrote: Are we supposed to assume that Ritsuko is not referring to Eva-01 when she says that Evas have salvaged souls? It's confusing because she says that Evas were made from Adam, without mentioning any exceptions. I don't know if the Eva01-Lilith connection is supposed to negate the other things that Ritsuko says about Evas, with respect to their applicability to Eva-01.

But it's clear that Gendo stops trying, for whatever reason, and instead co-opts 3I with hopes that he can go to her. We can probably assume that Gend

We can probably assume that Gendo is not an idiot, merely desperate to be with his beloved again. He probably settles on the "go to Yui" plan for a good reason. It's also important to remember that Yui and her two compadres had some sort of plan in place before Yui's "accident", and this is presumably still being taken forward at the same time as Gendo's more private ambition. So it's not just a matter of getting Yui back (/being with her again); Gendo, Fuyu, and Yui have to pull off whatever it is they've been planning for some 15 years, as well. So, if you really think Gendo isn't an idiot, then you'll give him enough credit to recognize that maybe (once he knew what was going on) he respected Yui's wishes enough to let her stay in the Eva and do what she needed to do. Plus what MonkEd says too. (In fact, considering what happens to Rei's soul when it gets transferred around...)
